channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Palestinian officials hail local elections in a Gaza community and the West Bank
item.description: Palestinian officials say local elections in Gaza and the West Bank mark a step toward a long-delayed presidential election. The Palestinian Authority hasn't held a presidential election in 21 years.
item.content:encoded:
Palestinian officials say local elections in Gaza and the West Bank mark a step toward a long-delayed presidential election. The Palestinian Authority hasn't held a presidential election in 21 years.

(Image credit: Mahmoud Illean)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Egypt's Grand Museum opens after many delays
item.description: We visit the new Grand Egyptian Museum, a spacious museum outside Cairo dedicated to displaying artifacts from ancient Egypt. It has finally opened its doors after decades of planning and construction.
item.content:encoded: We visit the new Grand Egyptian Museum, a spacious museum outside Cairo dedicated to displaying artifacts from ancient Egypt. It has finally opened its doors after decades of planning and construction.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: How China is responding to the stalling of talks between the U.S. and Iran
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks to Zongyuan Zoe Liu, a senior fellow at the Council on Foreign Relations, about how China views the current crisis in the Middle East
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks to Zongyuan Zoe Liu, a senior fellow at the Council on Foreign Relations, about how China views the current crisis in the Middle East
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: Trump doubts shooter motivated by Iran war as peace talks on hold
item.description: A shooting incident at the White House Correspondent's Dinner took focus away from the war in Iran, as Iran's foreign minister planned to return to Islamabad, the site of previous peace talks.
item.content:encoded:
A shooting incident at the White House Correspondent's Dinner took focus away from the war in Iran, as Iran's foreign minister planned to return to Islamabad, the site of previous peace talks.

(Image credit: Meysam Mirzadeh)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: King Charles U.S. visit comes at tense moment in transatlantic relationship
item.description: King Charles and Queen Camilla arrive Monday for a four day U.S. state visit. Some hope the royal touch can heal the transatlantic rift that's emerged under Trump.
item.content:encoded:
King Charles and Queen Camilla arrive Monday for a four day U.S. state visit. Some hope the royal touch can heal the transatlantic rift that's emerged under Trump.

(Image credit: Yui Mok/AP)
item.dc:creator: Fatima Al-Kassab

item.title: In a rare interview, a leader of the world's largest right-wing group talks to NPR
item.description: The second-in-command of the RSS, a Hindu nationalist organization in India, rarely speaks to the Western press. Here's what he said about his group's controversial history.
item.content:encoded:
The second-in-command of the RSS, a Hindu nationalist organization in India, rarely speaks to the Western press. Here's what he said about his group's controversial history.

(Image credit: IDREES MOHAMMED)
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: Mali reeling after coordinated attacks hit multiple cities
item.description: Gunfire and explosions rocked Mali's capital and other key cities in one of the most significant coordinated attacks in years, as armed groups exploited worsening insecurity in the Sahel region.
item.content:encoded:
Gunfire and explosions rocked Mali's capital and other key cities in one of the most significant coordinated attacks in years, as armed groups exploited worsening insecurity in the Sahel region.

(Image credit: uncredited)
item.dc:creator: Jewel Bright

item.title: Iran's foreign minister leaves Pakistan, then Trump cancels U.S. delegation's travel
item.description: After arriving on Friday, Iran's Abbas Araghchi has left Islamabad, prompting President Trump to announce that his envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will no longer travel there Saturday for peace talks
item.content:encoded:
After arriving on Friday, Iran's Abbas Araghchi has left Islamabad, prompting President Trump to announce that his envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will no longer travel there Saturday for peace talks

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Vessels seized by the U.S. this week were part of a global 'shadow fleet'
item.description: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Elisabeth Braw, senior fellow at the Atlantic Council, about shadow vessels, after the U.S. military's seizure of two Iranian-linked oil tankers.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Elisabeth Braw, senior fellow at the Atlantic Council, about shadow vessels, after the U.S. military's seizure of two Iranian-linked oil tankers.
item.dc:creator: Kathryn Fink

item.title: Kushner and U.S. envoy Witkoff will head to Pakistan for new Iran talks
item.description: Iran's foreign minister arrived in Islamabad, and the White House says Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will go there Saturday to try to "move the ball forward towards a deal."
item.content:encoded:
Iran's foreign minister arrived in Islamabad, and the White House says Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will go there Saturday to try to "move the ball forward towards a deal."

(Image credit: Farooq Naeem)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff
